rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 7, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT EVELYN HOM's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint is uncertain. The allegations in the FAC (paragraph 4 and 14a) suggest that Katie Hom, as individual entered into the Lease and Settlement Agreement as owner and landlord; the allegations further suggest that Trust succeeded to Katie Hom, as individual, rights and obligations as owner and landlord upon her death. However, Ex. 2 to Plaintiff's RJN request suggest that the time of the Lease, Hom Trust was the owner of the property and the lease was entered into by "original trustees of the trust" in their capacity as trustee. Plaintiff has a final leave to amend to allege in good faith that (1) Evelyn Hom, as trustee, is a successor to Katie Hom, as individual, as landlord and owner; and/or (2) to allege that Katie Hom entered into Lease and/or Settlement Agreement as trustee/on behalf of the trust and Evelyn Hom, as a successor trustee is a successor in rights and liabilities. Plaintiff shall make allegations in good faith and not in violation of sham pleading doctrine. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM O'NEILL ET AL VS. PIERRE BERTERRETCHE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Pierre Berterretche, Marie Berterretche, Dominic Berterretche, And Marie Elissetche'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 7, 2017, line 4. Defendants Pierre Berterretche, Marie Berterretche, Dominic Berterretche, And Marie Elissetche'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions DENIED. The motion was not filed within the jurisdictional time limit. (Vidal Sassoon, Inc. v. Superior Court (1983) 147. App. 3d 681.) Response containing only objections need not be verified. (Blue Ridge Ins, Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 202 CA3d 339, 344.) No sanctions. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM O'NEILL ET AL VS. PIERRE BERTERRETCHE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Adam O'Neill And Maria O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 7, 2017, line 4. Plaintiffs Adam O'Neill And Maria O'Neill'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED as follows: defendant shall within 30 days serve substantive responses to: Special Interrogatories 6, 7, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 19, 20, 21, 34, 35; and Form Interrogatories 12.4, 12.5, 13.1, and 13.2. Defendants shall provide a further substantive response to Request for Admission 3, 9, 11, and 13. Defendants shall within 30 days serve a further response to plaintiffs' Requests for Production of Documents responsive to Requests 5, 9, 11, 12, 13, 17, 19, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 29, 35, 36, 39, 41, and 42. Defendants shall state that they do not have and never have had, or if they have, why they no longer have requested documents. "Not Applicable" or "N/A" is not responsive. Defendants shall serve all responsive documents it has within 30 days. Defendant's Preliminary Statement and General Objections are ordered stricken as to all interrogatories and document demands. (See Rutter Guide, Civ. Pro. Before Trial at sec. 8:1071. "Objections: In lieu of answering or allowing inspection of records, above, the responding party may serve objections. Each objection must be stated separately (no objections to entire set), and must bear the same number or letter as the interrogatory to which it is directed. [CCP § 2030.210(a)(3)] Objections must be specific. A motion to compel lies where objections are "too general." [CCP § 2030.300(a)(3); see Korea Data Systems Co. Ltd. v. Sup.Ct. (Amazing Technologies Corp.) (1997) 51 CA4th 1513, 1516, 59 CR2d 925, 926-objecting party subject to sanctions for "boilerplate" objections." The same rule applies to objections to document demands- see CCP §2031.210.) No sanctions. = (501/REQ)
